growth_records.cite: Sharma et al. Spatial segregation and cooperation in radially expanding microbial colonies under antibiotic stress. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-00982-2. [PMC8443724]
growth_records.snippet: the slope of the appearance time vs. log(density) plot provides an estimate of 1/k. For the conditions we measured, k ~ 0.02 min-1, which corresponds to an approximate doubling time of 35 min, only slightly slower than drug-free growth in l
growth_records.notes: Effective per-capita exponential growth rate k ~ 0.02 min-1 derived from slope of colony appearance-time vs log(inoculum density). growth_rate_per_h = 0.02 min-1 x 60 = 1.2 h-1. Doubling time = ln2/0.02 = 34.7 min = 0.578 h (paper states ~35 min). Measured on 1% BHI agar at 37 C, both with and without super-MIC ampicillin (expansion velocity approx invariant across drug conditions). No substrate uptake or product secretion measured. Colony/range-expansion growth on solid agar (nearest culture_mode = batch).

growth_records.medium: Human colonic organoid (colonoid) natively formed mucus, no exogenous nutrients (bacteria inoculated in a salt solution)

up.units: not quantified; bacteria grow on host-derived nutrients with no exogenous carbon added

growth_records.cite: Mikhaleva, Sofya et al. Adaptation of Enterococcus faecalis to intestinal mucus revealed by a human colonic organoid model. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01304-25. [PMC13098192]
growth_records.snippet: After 6 h, the number of viable Ef cells increased approximately 50-fold, indicating favorable growth conditions. This corresponds to a doubling time of ~1 h, which is slower than in the brain-heart infusion (BHI) medium, suggesting that Ef
growth_records.notes: Only quantitative growth parameter in the paper. ~50-fold increase in viable CFU over 6 h. Paper states doubling time ~1 h. Cross-check: mu = ln(50)/6 h = 3.912/6 = 0.652 /h; doubling time = ln2/0.652 = 1.06 h (~1 h, consistent with authors). growth_rate_per_h reported here as 0.65 (calculated from 50-fold/6 h); doubling_time 1.0 h as stated. No uptake or secretion rates measured (Tn-seq + live imaging study). culture_mode set to 'co-culture' as the strict list has no 'organoid/tissue-associated' option; it is a bacterium-on-host-tissue association. Medium is native colonoid mucus, composition not defined. BHI comparison is from a cited reference (#42), not measured here, so not recorded.

growth_records.cite: Weiss, Anna S. et al. In vitro interaction network of a synthetic gut bacterial community. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01153-z. [PMC8941000]
growth_records.snippet: Strains were grouped by growth rate (GR) into fast growing strains (GR > 1.5 h-1, E. faecalis KB1, B. animalis YL2, C. innocuum I46 and B. coccoides YL58) ... lactate production was confirmed for E. faecalis KB1 ... E. faecalis KB1 depleted
growth_records.notes: Growth rate stated only as a threshold bin (fast, GR>1.5 h-1); exact per-strain value is in Table S2 (supplement not available here), so 1.5 h-1 is a LOWER BOUND, not the measured value. Secretion/uptake are qualitative (produced/consumed/depleted) from untargeted MS of spent AF medium; no numeric mmol/gDW/h rates reported in main text. E. faecalis KB1 acidified medium (pH_SM<6.2). No Yxs/qS derivable (no biomass or substrate mass balance given).

growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Enterococcus faecalis KB1 (Firmicutes), Max OD 1.78, Mean μ ± SD 2.5 ± 0.01.
growth_records.notes: μ=2.5 h^-1 (±0.01) reported — fastest grower in the consortium. td = ln2/μ = 0.6931/2.5 = 0.277 h. Max OD600 = 1.78. Arginine supplementation did not improve growth (text).

growth_records.cite: Anthony O. Gaca et al. Basal Levels of (p)ppGpp in Enterococcus faecalis: the Magic beyond the Stringent Response. mBio (2013). DOI:10.1128/mBio.00646-13. [PMC3781836]
growth_records.snippet: cells were grown at 37°C in the chemically defined medium FMC ... supplemented with 10 mM glucose (FMCG) ... grown to an OD600 of ~0.25, and labeled ... until each culture reached an OD600 of 0.4 (~50 min for the OG1RF and ∆relQ strains ...
growth_records.notes: Growth rate CALCULATED from the labeling window OD600 0.25->0.40 in ~50 min: µ = ln(0.40/0.25)/(50/60 h) = ln(1.6)/0.8333 = 0.4700/0.8333 = 0.564 h-1; doubling_time = ln2/0.564 = 1.23 h. Wild-type E. faecalis is homolactic in FMCG; lactate/formate/ethanol/acetoin measured only as concentration-vs-OD curves (Fig 3), no numeric values or biomass-specific rates in text, so secretion rate=null. pH not fixed (falls during growth). Low-phosphate FMCG used for the labeling that defines this growth window.

growth_records.cite: Anthony O. Gaca et al. Basal Levels of (p)ppGpp in Enterococcus faecalis: the Magic beyond the Stringent Response. mBio (2013). DOI:10.1128/mBio.00646-13. [PMC3781836]
growth_records.snippet: the Δrsh strain showed a slow-growth phenotype in rich medium ... (~70 min for the Δrsh strain). ... basal levels of ppGpp were approximately 4-fold higher in the Δrsh strain ... accompanied by an ~40% reduction in GTP.
growth_records.notes: µ CALCULATED from OD600 0.25->0.40 in ~70 min: µ = ln(1.6)/(70/60) = 0.4700/1.1667 = 0.403 h-1; td = ln2/0.403 = 1.72 h. Slow-growth phenotype confirmed by the longest labeling window; consistent with ~4-fold elevated basal ppGpp and ~40% lower GTP vs WT. No fermentation rates or yields reported for this strain.

growth_records.strain: OG1RF (with pBSU101 derivative plasmids: sensitive pBSU101-DasherGFP, resistant pBSU101-BFP-BL expressing beta-lactamase)
growth_records.cite: Hallinen et al. Delayed antibiotic exposure induces population collapse in enterococcal communities with drug-resistant subpopulations. eLife (2020). DOI:10.7554/eLife.52813. [PMC7159880]
growth_records.snippet: we find that drug-free growth rate under the current experimental conditions is approximately g_max = 1 hr-1 ... the maximum bacterial growth rate under these conditions, which corresponds to a doubling time of approximately 30-40 min
growth_records.notes: E. faecalis OG1RF (fully sequenced oral isolate) drug-free maximum per-capita growth rate g_max approx 1 h-1 in BHI at 37C, measured from bioreactor media flow rate required to hold constant density. Doubling time stated 30-40 min (0.50-0.667 h); reported ~0.58 h midpoint. Cross-check: ln2/1.0=0.693 h=41.6 min, consistent with the upper end of the stated 30-40 min range. No metabolic uptake/secretion or MFA fluxes reported (antibiotic population-dynamics study). Same drug-free rate applies to both engineered sensitive (pBSU101-DasherGFP) and resistant (pBSU101-BFP-BL, beta-lactamase) OG1RF derivatives. Minor recipe inconsistency in text: overnight cultures noted with streptomycin 120 ug/mL while plasmids carry spectinomycin resistance.

yields.Yxs_note: Y = 0.4 (dimensionless) NOT measured here; set from typical literature values for bacteria to run the Novick-Szilard chemostat model

growth_records.cite: Legner et al. Role of Dilution Rate and Nutrient Availability in the Formation of Microbial Biofilms. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00916. [PMC6503106]
growth_records.snippet: we used two constants, mu_max (1.07 h-1) and Ks (0.019) derived from our E. faecalis static cultures... The parameters mu_max and Ks were calculated from a double reciprocal plot of mu and S (dilution of TSB) values.
growth_records.notes: mu_max = 1.07 h-1 is an EXPERIMENTALLY DERIVED value: individual specific growth rates were measured from OD600 slopes (readings every 30 min at 37C) across a two-fold TSB dilution series, then extrapolated to mu_max via a Monod/double-reciprocal fit; it is an extrapolated maximum, not a single measured culture. Ks = 0.019 in arbitrary units (fraction of full-strength TSB), so no absolute substrate concentration. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693/1.07 = 0.648 h. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates were measured (empty). Yxs = 0.4 was assumed from literature, not measured (flagged in yields). Same strain later used in the chemostat runs (separate records).

growth_records.cite: Legner et al. Role of Dilution Rate and Nutrient Availability in the Formation of Microbial Biofilms. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00916. [PMC6503106]
growth_records.snippet: Three different dilution rates (D) were used to establish steady-state cultures: 0.09, 0.28, and 0.81 h-1... it was set to maintain the dilution rate D at 0.81, 0.28, and 0.09 h-1, respectively.
growth_records.notes: At chemostat steady state the planktonic specific growth rate mu equals the dilution rate D, so mu = 0.09 h-1. Doubling time reported directly in Table 3 = 7.7 h (consistent with ln2/0.09 = 7.70 h). Table 3 lists this as 8.4% of mu_max (0.09/1.07 = 8.4%). Substrate is 1/16 TSB (glucose 28 mM and 66 mM phosphate held constant; peptone components diluted 16-fold: 17/16=1.0625, 3/16=0.1875, 5/16=0.3125 g/L). No uptake or secretion rates measured. Biofilm density at this D was 3.78e6 cells/cm2 (measured by CLSM, not a rate).

growth_records.cite: Legner et al. Role of Dilution Rate and Nutrient Availability in the Formation of Microbial Biofilms. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00916. [PMC6503106]
growth_records.snippet: Three different dilution rates (D) were used to establish steady-state cultures: 0.09, 0.28, and 0.81 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Steady-state mu = D = 0.28 h-1. Doubling time in Table 3 = 2.5 h (ln2/0.28 = 2.48 h). Table 3 lists 26.1% of mu_max (0.28/1.07 = 26.2%). Medium 1/16 TSB as above. No uptake/secretion rates measured. Measured biofilm density 5.54e7 cells/cm2 (CLSM, not a rate).

growth_records.cite: Legner et al. Role of Dilution Rate and Nutrient Availability in the Formation of Microbial Biofilms. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00916. [PMC6503106]
growth_records.snippet: Three different dilution rates (D) were used to establish steady-state cultures: 0.09, 0.28, and 0.81 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Steady-state mu = D = 0.81 h-1. Doubling time in Table 3 = 0.9 h (ln2/0.81 = 0.856 h). Table 3 lists 75.6% of mu_max (0.81/1.07 = 75.7%). Medium 1/16 TSB as above. No uptake/secretion rates measured. Measured biofilm density 7.94e7 cells/cm2 (CLSM). Over the D range biofilm was 21-fold denser at a 9-fold higher D.

growth_records.cite: Cermak, Nathan et al. High-throughput measurement of single-cell growth rates using serial microfluidic mass sensor arrays. Nature Biotechnology (2016). DOI:10.1038/nbt.3666. [PMC5064867]
growth_records.snippet: This strain behaved similarly to the gram-negative rod E. coli in the serial SMR array, growing at easily-detectable rates with a mean growth rate of 1.86 +/- 0.02/h.
growth_records.notes: Single-cell exponential growth rate measured directly by serial SMR array; mu = 1.86 +/- 0.02 /h reported (E. faecalis, Supplementary Fig. 12). Doubling time not reported; CALCULATED td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/1.86 = 0.3727 h = 22.4 min (growth_method reported for mu, doubling calculated). No uptake/secretion/MFA/yield data reported. E. faecalis grown overnight in Brain Heart Infusion (BHI, Difco) at 37C, diluted 100,000-fold in BHI + 0.2% Tween-80, incubated 3 h at 37C before loading. Base BHI composition not stated -> medium_composition_stated false. pH not stated.
